name: Zabbix MCP Server
what_it_does: MCP server for Zabbix — the open-source enterprise infrastructure monitoring platform. Enables AI agents to query Zabbix for host status, triggers, alerts, item data, graphs, and event history — supporting AI-driven operations monitoring, anomaly detection, and automated incident response workflows.
use_cases: Querying Zabbix for current host and service status from operations monitoring agents
Retrieving triggered alerts and recent events from IT operations agents
Analyzing Zabbix metric history for anomaly detection by AI operations agents
Automating incident response workflows by integrating Zabbix alerts with AI decision systems
not_for: Teams not using Zabbix (Nagios, Datadog, Prometheus/Grafana users need different MCPs)
Cloud-only monitoring without on-premise Zabbix deployment
Real-time streaming metrics (Zabbix API is request-response, not streaming)
best_when: An enterprise IT team using Zabbix for infrastructure monitoring wants AI agents to query monitoring data, correlate alerts, and assist with incident response and capacity planning.
avoid_when: You use Prometheus, Datadog, Nagios, or cloud-native monitoring. Also: Zabbix API access requires Zabbix server setup.

auth.notes: Zabbix API token or username/password for authentication. Zabbix 5.4+ supports API tokens. Use read-only Zabbix user for agent access.

pricing.paid_tiers: Zabbix Enterprise Support (optional) — software is free
pricing.notes: Zabbix is free open source (GPL). Commercial support optional. MCP server is free open source.

performance.rate_limits: Zabbix API has no explicit rate limits — bounded by server performance

known_agent_gotchas: Zabbix API versions differ across releases — verify API version compatibility with your Zabbix server
Large Zabbix deployments have millions of items — be specific with queries to avoid timeouts
Zabbix API token vs user/password auth differs by version — check your Zabbix version
Read-only Zabbix API user recommended — prevents accidental host/trigger modifications
agent_readiness.security_notes: Zabbix contains sensitive infrastructure data. Use read-only API access. TLS to Zabbix server recommended. Zabbix credentials must be secured.

security_score_components.security_notes: Infrastructure monitoring data is sensitive. Read-only access recommended. TLS. Secure Zabbix credentials.
